top_issues.issue: Large Module / God File: 5669 lines covering 70+ distinct register_* functions spanning buffer, cursor, theme, hooks, keymaps, panels, strings, lists, filesystem and focus primitives. All Lisp–Rust bridging lives in one file.

Large Module: 6882 lines. Though 60% is test code, the production run() function contains a long event loop with SaveBuffer, OpenFile, ExecCommand, Eval, and SaveAndQuit arms that could be extracted to a dispatcher module.

Large Struct (God Object tendency): EditorState carries 35+ public fields covering buffer, cursor, viewport, registers, undo/redo stacks, macro system, jump list, change list, theme, named_themes, panels, line styles, line backgrounds, keymaps, hooks, and focus state. Single Responsibility is strained.

Long Method: handle_key_event() spans ~400 lines with 12 distinct if-let / match branches (one per InputState variant), each containing non-trivial logic. The function handles macro recording, command mode, search mode, PendingChar, PendingReplace, PendingMark, PendingJumpMark, PendingRegister, PendingMacroRecord, PendingMacroPlay, OperatorPending, and TextObject states.

Duplicated Code: argument-extraction pattern (match args.get(N) / Some(Value::Int(n)) / Some(other) / None returning RuntimeError) is repeated ~20 times across register_set_line_style, register_set_line_background, register_define_panel, register_set_panel_line, etc. extract_string_arg and extract_string_arg_at helpers exist but integer/bool extraction is not abstracted.

Feature Envy / Insider Trading: the run() event loop directly accesses state_rc.borrow().buffer.version(), state_rc.borrow().cursor, state_rc.borrow().mode, and state_rc.borrow().message across multiple borrows for change-detection, rather than a single snapshot or dedicated change-set return value from handle_key_event.

Tramp Data / Workaround: Dynamic Lisp command handlers receive a dummy EditorState (alfred_core::editor_state::new(1,1)) because the real state is captured by the Rc<RefCell> closure. This is a workaround for the architectural tension between the CommandHandler trait expecting &mut EditorState and Lisp closures needing Rc<RefCell>.

Divergent Change: EditorState must be modified when adding new editor features (macro system, jump list, change list, panels, line styles) AND when adding new Lisp primitives, AND when the rendering pipeline needs new state. Any one domain concern forces changes to this single struct.

Shotgun Surgery: adding a new Lisp primitive requires (1) a new register_X function in bridge.rs, (2) a call added to the appropriate register_X_primitives() grouper, and (3) the grouper called in main.rs. The registration-call chain is spread across three locations.
Dead Code (unused variable): let _lisp_env = runtime.env() on line 134 of bridge.rs (register_define_command) is assigned but never used — comment says 'no longer used'.
Dead Module: rainbow_csv.rs exists as a file in alfred-core/src but is not exported from lib.rs (not listed in pub mod declarations). The module is orphaned from the crate's public API.
Magic Numbers: hardcoded poll timeout of 100ms (Duration::from_millis(100)) and fallback terminal size (80, 24) are inline literals with no named constant.

Coupling via Direct Field Access: handle_key_event accesses 15+ distinct EditorState fields directly (macro_recording, macro_replaying, macro_buffer, pending_register, last_char_find, search_pattern, search_forward, etc.) creating wide structural coupling between the input module and the state model.

Inconsistent Naming: register_string_primitives and register_list_primitives use NativeFunc (plain fn pointers registered directly on env) while all other register_X functions use define_native_closure with closures. This stylistic inconsistency makes the bridge harder to navigate.
